steps.title: Identify the pronoun reference signal
steps.content: The key signal is the pronoun they, which must refer clearly to commercial plastics. The passage describes how they are made from nonrenewable petroleum and do not biodegrade—facts about plastics, not problems. For they to have an unambiguous antecedent, the main clause must establish commercial plastics as the grammatical subject.

steps.title: Apply the pronoun reference rule
steps.content: Choice D makes commercial plastics the grammatical subject with have two associated problems:, ensuring the pronoun they clearly refers back to plastics. Choices A and B place problems as the grammatical focus, creating ambiguous pronoun reference (do the problems themselves get made from petroleum?), and choice C uses awkward are that phrasing instead of a clear construction. This matches choice D — D is correct.
